notes: Watched the second part of the React Course on State. I found out that there's something called array destructuring. We use "React.useState()", to help us keep track of state in a function. The line returns an array wherein the first element is the current data, and the second is a functionwhose sole job is to update the data. It is often caled the updater function. The convention in naming it is is that you use setAndWhatEverTheNameIs. There's so much work to do.
